title: VTEP Reachability and Loss
exclusions: Pure EVPN neighbor-state dashboards without NVE counter or RTT lanes (UC-18.3.14); BGP RIB-only convergence without dataplane loss (UC-18.3.15); hardware TCAM or ACL-only telemetry lacking VTEP peers (UC-18.3.20); ACI APIC fault-only views without NX-OS NVE exports when fabrics are standalone Nexus.
evidence: Saved searches vtep_loss_core and vtep_loss_alert plus Dashboard Studio workspace VTEP Loss & Latency
dataSources: `index=dc_fabric` / `index=network_ops` / `index=network` — five sourcetypes: **`nxos:nve_peers`** (**`show nve peers detail`** JSON: **peer_ip**, **vtep_ip**, **vni**, state); **`nxos:nve_counters`** from **`show nve interface`** (**encap_errors**, **decap_errors**); **`network:vxlan_tunnel`** (**tunnel_state**, **loss_pct**, **rtt_ms**); **`cisco:nxos:syslog`** (**NVE-**, **VTEP**, **L2RIB** strings); **`network:ping_results`** from **`ping nve`** (**rtt_ms**, **loss_pct**). Ingest via Splunk REST API Modular Input ([Splunkbase **1546**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1546)), Cisco Data Center Networking ([Splunkbase **7777**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/7777)) reference dashboards, Splunk Add-on for Cisco ACI ([Splunkbase 1897](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1897)) for hybrid sites only, Splunk Add-on for VMware NSX ([Splunkbase 4241](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4241)) and Splunk Add-on for VMware ([Splunkbase 3215](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/3215)) when compute motion explains asymmetric paths.
app: **Splunk REST API Modular Input** ([Splunkbase **1546**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1546)); **Cisco Data Center Networking for Splunk Platform** ([Splunkbase **7777**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/7777)); **Splunk Add-on for Cisco ACI** ([Splunkbase 1897](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1897)); **Splunk Add-on for VMware NSX** ([Splunkbase 4241](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4241)); **Splunk Add-on for VMware** ([Splunkbase 3215](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/3215)).
spl:
(index=dc_fabric OR index=network_ops OR index=network)
(sourcetype="nxos:nve_peers" OR sourcetype="nxos:nve_counters" OR sourcetype="network:vxlan_tunnel" OR sourcetype="cisco:nxos:syslog" OR sourcetype="network:ping_results")
earliest=-4h@m
| eval leaf=trim(coalesce(host,device_name,switch_name,nms_hostname,router_id,""))
| eval fabric_site=trim(coalesce(site_code,fabric_site,pod_id,"unspecified"))
| eval peer_ip=trim(coalesce(peer_ip,nve_peer_ip,remote_vtep,vtep_peer,""))
| eval vtep_ip=trim(coalesce(vtep_ip,src_vtep,local_nve_ip,nve_source_ip,""))
| eval rtt_ms=tonumber(coalesce(rtt_ms,avg_rtt,ping_rtt_ms,icmp_rtt,latency_ms,""))
| eval loss_pct=tonumber(coalesce(loss_pct,packet_loss_pct,icmp_loss_pct,ploss,""))
| eval encap_errors=tonumber(coalesce(encap_errors,nve_encap_err,encap_drops,""))
| eval decap_errors=tonumber(coalesce(decap_errors,nve_decap_err,decap_drops,""))
| eval vni=tonumber(coalesce(vni,l2vni,bd_vni,""))
| eval tunnel_health=lower(trim(coalesce(tunnel_state,vxlan_state,path_state,"")))
| eval nve_peer_down=if(sourcetype="nxos:nve_peers" AND match(lower(_raw),"(down|failed|init|unknown)"),1,0)
| eval loss_breach=if(sourcetype="network:ping_results" AND (loss_pct>1 OR (isnotnull(loss_pct) AND loss_pct>=0.5 AND isnotnull(rtt_ms) AND rtt_ms>25)),1,0)
| eval latency_breach=if(sourcetype="network:ping_results" AND isnotnull(rtt_ms) AND rtt_ms>30,1,0)
| eval counter_breach=if(sourcetype="nxos:nve_counters" AND ((isnotnull(encap_errors) AND encap_errors>0) OR (isnotnull(decap_errors) AND decap_errors>0)),1,0)
| eval agg_tunnel_bad=if(sourcetype="network:vxlan_tunnel" AND match(tunnel_health,"(down|degraded|failed|unknown)"),1,0)
| eval syslog_nve_fault=if(sourcetype="cisco:nxos:syslog" AND match(_raw,"(?i)NVE-\\d|%NVE-|nve peer|vtep"),1,0)
| where match(leaf,".+")
| fillnull value=0 nve_peer_down loss_breach latency_breach counter_breach agg_tunnel_bad syslog_nve_fault
| eval signal=nve_peer_down*5 + loss_breach*3 + latency_breach*2 + counter_breach*4 + agg_tunnel_bad*4 + syslog_nve_fault*3
| bin _time span=10m
| stats max(rtt_ms) AS worst_rtt_ms max(loss_pct) AS worst_loss_pct sum(encap_errors) AS encap_sum sum(decap_errors) AS decap_sum max(signal) AS signal_peak values(peer_ip) AS peers values(vtep_ip) AS local_vteps values(sourcetype) AS sourcetypes_seen dc(sourcetype) AS st_diversity BY _time leaf fabric_site vni
| where signal_peak>=3 OR worst_loss_pct>=1 OR worst_rtt_ms>=35 OR encap_sum>500 OR decap_sum>500
| eval story=leaf."|vni=".if(isnotnull(vni),tostring(vni),"any")."|loss=".tostring(round(coalesce(worst_loss_pct,0)))."|rtt=".tostring(round(coalesce(worst_rtt_ms,0)))
| sort - signal_peak - _time
| head 200
| table _time fabric_site leaf vni peers local_vteps worst_rtt_ms worst_loss_pct encap_sum decap_sum signal_peak sourcetypes_seen story
description: Correlates NX-OS NVE peer detail, NVE interface counters, normalized VXLAN tunnel health, NVE syslog faults, and orchestrated ping nve results so operators see packet loss, RTT inflation, and encapsulation errors across VTEPs and VNIs—not only peer Up/Down state.
value: East-west revenue workloads ride the same VXLAN mesh; small sustained loss or asymmetric latency shows up as intermittent SQL timeouts, storage retries, and unexplained help-desk bursts long after BGP neighbors look Established. Quantifying loss_pct and rtt_ms per fabric_site gives SRE and capacity teams evidence to prioritize optics, queue tuning, or automation fixes before executives see SLA rebates.
implementation: Poll NX-API for show nve peers detail into nxos:nve_peers and show nve interface into nxos:nve_counters; emit network:vxlan_tunnel health tuples; keep cisco:nxos:syslog for NVE faults; land network:ping_results from ping nve automation. FIELDALIAS peer_ip, vtep_ip, rtt_ms, loss_pct, encap_errors, decap_errors, vni. Maintain vtep_loss_thresholds.csv. Schedule vtep_loss_core and vtep_loss_alert every five minutes with throttles.
visualization: (1) Heat map — VTEP × VTEP loss_pct from network:ping_results. (2) Line chart — perc95(rtt_ms) by fabric_site. (3) Table — top signal_peak with peers and vni. (4) Single value — encap/decap breach counts from nxos:nve_counters.
schema: CIM Network_Traffic for ping and tunnel-derived path metrics with peer_ip endpoints; Performance for rtt_ms, loss_pct, and counters when mapped as time-series KPIs.
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Performance subsets only after rtt_ms and loss_pct extractions stabilize across NX-OS trains; keep raw nxos:nve_peers samples unaccelerated until API schemas freeze.

references.title: IETF RFC 8365 — Network Virtualization Overlay Using EVPN
Cisco Nexus 9000 NX-OS VXLAN BGP EVPN Configuration Guide
Cisco Data Center Networking for Splunk (Splunkbase 7777)

knownFalsePositives:
**mt_scheduled_ping** — Maintenance windows sometimes pause orchestrated **`ping nve`** jobs while data plane keeps forwarding; Splunk shows stale **`network:ping_results`** gaps that look like loss until `_indextime` proves the probe stopped. Pair gaps with change tickets before paging overlay owners.

**icmp_priority_deny** — Control-plane policers or CoPP can silently drop ICMP toward VTEP loopbacks even when VXLAN encapsulation is healthy; `worst_rtt_ms` spikes without `encap_errors` growth often trace to classification updates rather than fabric faults. Validate with scripted TCP or BFD-sidecars before blaming underlay optics.

**counter_reset_window** — `show nve interface` counter clears during linecard restarts produce synthetic decap deceleration in `nxos:nve_counters` for a single poll; ignore single-bin decap_sum spikes when syslog shows %CMGR- or module reload markers in the same minute bucket.

**lldp_hostname_drift** — ZTP or partial bootstrap can leave syslog hostnames mismatched to NX-API `host` fields until DNS catches up; duplicate leaf rows or missing joins mimic VTEP skew until normalization macros align identities.

**asymmetric_ecmp_probe** — Hash-selected ICMP paths may traverse colder uplinks while production VXLAN flows stay balanced; loss_pct from probes can over-state tenant impact unless you compare against bidirectional samples or multi-source pings.

**sup_spine_upgrade** — Planned spine rotations flood transient NVE syslog warnings as RIBs relearn even though tunnel endpoints stay Up; widen suppression when CAB references SUP spine change tokens without dataplane blackholes.

**telemetry_batching_skew** — Heavy forwarders batching multiple polls into one second can compress `loss_pct` math; when in doubt, compare against CLI `ping nve` manual spot checks for the same tuple.

**duplicate_collector_polls** — Two modular inputs hitting the same leaf double encap_sum/decap_sum and inflate `signal_peak`. Run forwarder audits whenever REST stanzas proliferate without ownership.
detailedImplementation:
**Prerequisites**
• Deploy Splunk REST API Modular Input ([Splunkbase **1546**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1546)) on hardened heavy forwarders; plan 120–300 second cadences per leaf so **show nve peers detail**, **show nve interface**, and **ping nve** transcripts stay poll-safe without NX-OS write tokens.
• Mirror starter macros from Cisco Data Center Networking for Splunk Platform ([Splunkbase **7777**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/7777)) only after index names and sourcetypes match this UC—rename macros to **dc_fabric**, **network_ops**, or **network** per governance.
• Add Splunk Add-on for Cisco ACI ([Splunkbase 1897](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1897)) only for brownfield fabrics bridging APIC with **VXLAN** leaves; keep APIC indexes separate from standalone EVPN telemetry.
• Add Splunk Add-on for VMware NSX ([Splunkbase 4241](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4241)) and Splunk Add-on for VMware ([Splunkbase 3215](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/3215)) when vMotion or Edge maintenance explains asymmetric **VTEP** paths.
• Grant collectors read-only RBAC for NX-API JSON or SSH automation that emits one JSON line per poll with **peer_ip**, **vtep_ip**, **vni**, **encap_errors**, **decap_errors**, **rtt_ms**, and **loss_pct** normalized to common field names.
• Document CoPP, CPU, and queue headroom: **nxos:nve_counters** spikes without ICMP loss often indicate NVE SAP drops, not underlay blackholes.
• Baseline **peer_ip** posture with **UC-18.3.9** before tightening loss thresholds; Up/Down health with climbing **loss_pct** or **rtt_ms** commonly reflects MTU drift, micro-bursts, or CoPP—not recycled BGP sessions alone.

**Step 1 — Configure data collection**
Index five sourcetypes into **dc_fabric**, **network_ops**, or **network**. For **nxos:nve_peers**, schedule NX-API HTTPS to **show nve peers detail** so each event carries peer state, **vni**, and pairing context for BGP EVPN neighbors. For **nxos:nve_counters**, poll **show nve interface** (JSON if available) for **encap_errors**, **decap_errors**, drops, and MTU pressure before silent brownouts. For **network:vxlan_tunnel**, flatten automation telemetry into **tunnel_state**, **path_state**, **fabric_site**, **loss_pct**, and **rtt_ms** after correlating BFD, ICMP, and device counters. For **cisco:nxos:syslog**, retain **NVE-** and **%NVE-** strings plus L2RIB hints that explain churn between API polls. For **network:ping_results**, run **ping nve** (or PBR-safe orchestrated ICMP) every five minutes with **source_leaf** and **target_peer_ip** tags so baselines survive ECMP hash changes. Expect low EPS per leaf with bursts during ISSU or spine work—throttle modular inputs per management VRF. Capture `_indextime` skew budgets whenever heavy forwarders batch polls so analysts can subtract transport delay from apparent loss.

**Step 2 — Create the search and alert**
Save **vtep_loss_core** from the SPL below on a five-minute schedule (fifteen minutes when API budgets tighten). Clone **vtep_loss_alert** with higher **signal_peak** cutoffs plus MaintenanceWindow lookups so ISSU windows do not storm on-call.

```spl
(index=dc_fabric OR index=network_ops OR index=network)
(sourcetype="nxos:nve_peers" OR sourcetype="nxos:nve_counters" OR sourcetype="network:vxlan_tunnel" OR sourcetype="cisco:nxos:syslog" OR sourcetype="network:ping_results")
earliest=-4h@m
| eval leaf=trim(coalesce(host,device_name,switch_name,nms_hostname,router_id,""))
| eval fabric_site=trim(coalesce(site_code,fabric_site,pod_id,"unspecified"))
| eval peer_ip=trim(coalesce(peer_ip,nve_peer_ip,remote_vtep,vtep_peer,""))
| eval vtep_ip=trim(coalesce(vtep_ip,src_vtep,local_nve_ip,nve_source_ip,""))
| eval rtt_ms=tonumber(coalesce(rtt_ms,avg_rtt,ping_rtt_ms,icmp_rtt,latency_ms,""))
| eval loss_pct=tonumber(coalesce(loss_pct,packet_loss_pct,icmp_loss_pct,ploss,""))
| eval encap_errors=tonumber(coalesce(encap_errors,nve_encap_err,encap_drops,""))
| eval decap_errors=tonumber(coalesce(decap_errors,nve_decap_err,decap_drops,""))
| eval vni=tonumber(coalesce(vni,l2vni,bd_vni,""))
| eval tunnel_health=lower(trim(coalesce(tunnel_state,vxlan_state,path_state,"")))
| eval nve_peer_down=if(sourcetype="nxos:nve_peers" AND match(lower(_raw),"(down|failed|init|unknown)"),1,0)
| eval loss_breach=if(sourcetype="network:ping_results" AND (loss_pct>1 OR (isnotnull(loss_pct) AND loss_pct>=0.5 AND isnotnull(rtt_ms) AND rtt_ms>25)),1,0)
| eval latency_breach=if(sourcetype="network:ping_results" AND isnotnull(rtt_ms) AND rtt_ms>30,1,0)
| eval counter_breach=if(sourcetype="nxos:nve_counters" AND ((isnotnull(encap_errors) AND encap_errors>0) OR (isnotnull(decap_errors) AND decap_errors>0)),1,0)
| eval agg_tunnel_bad=if(sourcetype="network:vxlan_tunnel" AND match(tunnel_health,"(down|degraded|failed|unknown)"),1,0)
| eval syslog_nve_fault=if(sourcetype="cisco:nxos:syslog" AND match(_raw,"(?i)NVE-\\d|%NVE-|nve peer|vtep"),1,0)
| where match(leaf,".+")
| fillnull value=0 nve_peer_down loss_breach latency_breach counter_breach agg_tunnel_bad syslog_nve_fault
| eval signal=nve_peer_down*5 + loss_breach*3 + latency_breach*2 + counter_breach*4 + agg_tunnel_bad*4 + syslog_nve_fault*3
| bin _time span=10m
| stats max(rtt_ms) AS worst_rtt_ms max(loss_pct) AS worst_loss_pct sum(encap_errors) AS encap_sum sum(decap_errors) AS decap_sum max(signal) AS signal_peak values(peer_ip) AS peers values(vtep_ip) AS local_vteps values(sourcetype) AS sourcetypes_seen dc(sourcetype) AS st_diversity BY _time leaf fabric_site vni
| where signal_peak>=3 OR worst_loss_pct>=1 OR worst_rtt_ms>=35 OR encap_sum>500 OR decap_sum>500
| eval story=leaf."|vni=".if(isnotnull(vni),tostring(vni),"any")."|loss=".tostring(round(coalesce(worst_loss_pct,0)))."|rtt=".tostring(round(coalesce(worst_rtt_ms,0)))
| sort - signal_peak - _time
| head 200
| table _time fabric_site leaf vni peers local_vteps worst_rtt_ms worst_loss_pct encap_sum decap_sum signal_peak sourcetypes_seen story
```

**Understanding this SPL**
Macro-wrap the index union as **%dc_fabric_idx%** so governance renames stay painless. The pipeline blends **nxos:nve_peers** state, **nxos:nve_counters** hardware counters, **network:vxlan_tunnel** aggregates, **cisco:nxos:syslog** faults, and **network:ping_results** RTT/loss—complementing **UC-18.3.9**, which emphasizes Up/Down without latency math. Coalesce aliases keep heterogeneous collectors aligned: **peer_ip** may arrive as **nve_peer_ip**; **rtt_ms** as **icmp_rtt**; **loss_pct** as **ploss**. Breach flags feed **signal** with weights favoring definitive peer-down states and counter breaches over soft ICMP noise. Ten-minute bins damp micro-bursts while staying SLA-relevant. Tune **worst_rtt_ms**, **worst_loss_pct**, and **encap_sum** using lookup **vtep_loss_thresholds.csv** (**fabric_site**, **rtt_ceiling**, **loss_ceiling**, **counter_floor**).

**Step 3 — Validate**
SSH to sample leaves and compare **show nve peers detail** against **nxos:nve_peers** events minute-for-minute, allowing one poll skew. Compare manual **ping nve** RTT to **network:ping_results** medians for the same source/target tuple. When Nexus Dashboard Fabric Controller or DCNM is deployed, compare with the DCNM overlay health UI first, then align Splunk vni rows to the same interval—discrepancies usually mean hostname drift between API and syslog exports. Validate **encap_errors** growth in **nxos:nve_counters** against CLI **show nve interface** deltas, not one-shot SNMP absolutes.

**Step 4 — Operationalize correlation**
Publish a runbook keyed from the **story** field: underlay owners for optics/ECMP, BGP EVPN owners for control-plane churn, NVE owners for counter symmetry. Ticket templates should carry **fabric_site**, **leaf**, **vni**, **peers**, **worst_loss_pct**, **worst_rtt_ms**, **encap_sum**, **decap_sum**, **sourcetypes_seen**, and **signal_peak** so post-incident reviews line up with TAC ShowTech timelines without ad-hoc CSV swaps.

**Step 5 — Visualization, alerting, and failure modes**
Visualization — In Dashboard Studio workspace **VTEP Loss & Latency**, use (1) a heat map of **worst_loss_pct** from **network:ping_results** across VTEP pairs; (2) a line chart of perc95(**worst_rtt_ms**) by **fabric_site** with change annotations; (3) a table ranked by **signal_peak** with peer columns; (4) single-value panels for **encap_sum**/**decap_sum** breaches sourced from **nxos:nve_counters**. Drill into UC-18.3.9 reachability boards when loss is flat but peers flap.

Alerting — Schedule **vtep_loss_alert** every five minutes; trigger when **signal_peak** ≥ 6 or **worst_loss_pct** ≥ 2 across two consecutive bins. Throttle 30 minutes per leaf and **fabric_site**. Suppress with **planned_change_window** rows tied to ISSU or spine tokens.

Failure modes — Missing **network:ping_results** removes RTT/loss authority—document the gap. If **network:ping_results** stops entirely after a credential rotation while other sourcetypes flow, modular input auth failed—renew secrets and confirm no splunkd.log HTTP 401 loops. Clock skew inflates **loss_pct**—audit NTP. Duplicate REST stanzas double counters—keep one input per leaf. When dashboards show fewer leaf hosts than CMDB expects, a retired forwarder silenced a poll path—reconcile inventory before tuning thresholds. Green dashboards with sick apps often mean microsegment ACLs block probes—pivot to endpoint traces before closing incidents.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the invisible tunnels between switches for sluggish replies and dropped ping tests so noisy fabrics get fixed before your apps stutter or time out.
hardware: Cisco Nexus 9000 NX-OS VXLAN BGP EVPN fabrics with optional VMware/NSX overlays
